behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: This can be caught by asserting the constructed API payload/prompt from a single call boundary. A real failure here is usually prompt/payload assembly drift, which does not require real I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Inject a fake API client, call classify(document), and assert the outgoing request contains every configured taxonomy label exactly once (or in the required serialized format).

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is output validation logic at the module boundary. A stubbed API response with an invalid label is sufficient to catch real failures in mapping/guarding behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Stub API response to an out-of-taxonomy label (for example, "medical") and assert classify(document) returns "unknown".

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: Real failure risk is model nondeterminism across repeated real model invocations. Demonstrating this requires multiple agentic steps in sequence (same input, repeated calls), which is Workflow by definition.
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated classify(document) calls against the real model/API under production-like settings and assert the returned label is stable across the sequence; fail on any divergence.

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: A true cost-control failure can come from mismatch between local estimates and provider token accounting. Catching that requires real API usage/tokens over real HTTP, not just in-process mocks.
behaviors.test_description: Send representative documents to the real API with production request construction, read provider-reported token usage, and assert usage <= configured per-call budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is retry orchestration across components (API adapter + retry/backoff + caller path). A simulated 429/RateLimit response with controlled time is enough; real network is not strictly required.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real retry stack with a fake API transport that returns rate-limit error then success; assert the module delays/backs off and eventually returns the successful label instead of surfacing an error.
